name: Commercial Real Estate Loans USA
description_short: Private commercial real estate lender offering mortgages, bridge loans, and hard money financing for projects from $100K to $2B nationwide and internationally.
description_long:
Commercial Real Estate Loans USA positions itself as a private lender specializing in commercial real estate financing that fills gaps left by traditional banks and mortgage lenders. The company claims to serve borrowers with commercial funding needs across multiple real estate sectors including multifamily, office buildings, and ground-up development projects, with geographic reach spanning worldwide markets.

The company offers a diverse range of financing products: commercial mortgages, commercial bridge loans (fundable in 5-7 days), commercial hard money loans (asset-based, up to 75% LTV), joint venture equity capital financing (up to 90% for large projects), mezzanine financing (1-5 business day closings), and commercial construction loans. Loan sizes range from $100,000 to $2 billion, with rates starting at 5% and LTV options up to 85%. Acceptable uses include acquisition, refinance, ground-up construction, renovations, equipment financing, and business working capital.

The company emphasizes speed and flexibility as distinguishing factors, highlighting rapid funding timelines (as few as 5 days for bridge loans) and willingness to finance projects that conventional lenders decline. They claim to provide "private commercial funding muscle" for deals banks won't touch, positioning themselves as specialists in time-sensitive transactions and non-traditional structures like joint ventures and mezzanine deals.

However, the website makes numerous unverified claims without third-party credentials, regulatory oversight details, or transparent pricing. Terms like "rates start at 5%" lack context regarding actual APRs, fees, or qualification requirements. No information exists about company background, licensing, loan performance data, or customer reviews. The aggressive marketing language ("virtually hassle-free," "amazing speed") lacks substantiation, and no specifics are provided about approval timelines, actual closing costs, or pre-qualification processes beyond a phone number and form.
pros: Wide loan size range from $100K to $2B accommodates various project scales
Fast funding claims for bridge loans (5-7 days) address time-sensitive deal needs
Multiple financing structures (mortgages, bridge, hard money, JV equity, mezzanine) provide options
Nationwide and international lending reach unlike regional competitors
Stated willingness to finance non-traditional projects conventional lenders reject
Loan-to-value up to 85% and hard money options for asset-based qualification
Specific use cases listed (acquisition, construction, renovations, equipment financing)
cons: Website provides no verifiable credentials, licensing information, or regulatory oversight details
Pricing entirely opaque—'rates start at 5%' lacks APR disclosure, fee structure, or qualification thresholds
No company background, founding date, management team, or third-party reviews provided
Unsubstantiated claims throughout ('world-class service,' 'record time,' 'virtually hassle-free') lack specifics
Pre-qualification and actual approval timeline processes unexplained; only phone number and form offered
best_for: Commercial real estate investors seeking bridge financing for time-sensitive acquisitions or auctions
Developers with ground-up construction projects rejected by traditional lenders
Borrowers with large loan requirements ($500M+) needing joint venture equity partners
diagnosis: Best suited for commercial real estate investors and developers with non-traditional projects, time constraints, or loan sizes/structures that conventional lenders decline. Primary caveat: the company lacks transparent pricing, regulatory credentials, and verifiable performance data, making it essential to verify claims independently and compare terms with licensed commercial lenders before committing.
